reasoning: `leads_to_self_temp` is external_body deriving `valid(p.leads_to(p))` which requires resolving nested `always(p.implies(eventually(p)))` closure gaps. This is an unverified trust assumption.
`leads_to_trans` is external_body composing two leads_to properties into a transitive chain through nested always/implies/eventually closures. This is a separate unverified trust assumption.
At n=0 the else branch fires and calls `leads_to_self_temp(p(0))` directly. There is no overflow — `n > 0` is false so `(n-1) as usize` is never evaluated. This is correct base case behavior.

Chaining usize::MAX steps is a correct instantiation of the verified inductive lemma. The decreases clause on usize is well-founded, and each step correctly applies. No new trust surface beyond φ1 and φ2.

Using `usize` instead of `nat` as the rank index silently bounds the ranking argument to `usize::MAX`. If a system requires a rank function exceeding platform word size, this lemma cannot express it — a subtle limitation compared to the nat-indexed variant that could mask liveness proof incompleteness.
